items.title: Why Most Process Improvements Fail Before They Start
items.summary: Lenny Rachitsky breaks down the most common reason operational change stalls: no one owns the outcome after the kickoff meeting. A practical framework for assigning accountability that actually sticks.

items.title: Claude Now Handles Multi-Step Workflows Without Human Checkpoints
items.summary: Anthropic's latest agent update enables Claude to execute complex multi-tool workflows end-to-end. For ops consultants, this changes what's automatable today versus what needed human review yesterday.

items.title: Build a Client Onboarding System in Notion in Under 2 Hours
items.summary: Step-by-step walkthrough of a relational Notion workspace for client onboarding: intake form, project database, SOP library, and automated status updates with no external tools required.

items.title: Small Business Owners Are Spending 11 Hours a Week on Tasks AI Could Handle
items.summary: New survey of 2,400 SMB owners puts a concrete number on the automation opportunity. The biggest time sinks: scheduling, status reporting, and invoice follow-up — all low-complexity, high-frequency.

items.title: n8n Raises $55M to Compete With Zapier on Enterprise Automation
items.summary: n8n's Series B signals that the no-code automation market is splitting into consumer-simple (Zapier) and developer-flexible (n8n). For consultants, this matters because clients will increasingly ask which tool to build on.

items.title: AI Assistants Are Making Middle Managers Worse, Not Better
items.summary: A contrarian read from MIT: AI tools that summarize meetings and generate status reports are creating a generation of managers who can produce the appearance of oversight without doing the thinking. Worth having in your back pocket when clients ask whether to automate their reporting.

items.title: The Constraint Theory Comeback: Why TOC Is the Framework for AI-Era Ops
items.summary: Process Street revisits Goldratt's Theory of Constraints and argues it's more relevant now that AI can clear bottlenecks instantly — making it critical to identify the right constraint before you automate anything.

items.title: Make.com vs n8n in 2026: An Honest Comparison for Non-Technical Teams
items.summary: Side-by-side breakdown across pricing, complexity ceiling, and reliability for SMB use cases. The verdict is more nuanced than most comparisons: Make.com wins on speed, n8n wins on flexibility above 20 steps.
